Title: The miR395b–ABI5 module regulates amylopectin branching and biosynthesis and affects lotus root quality

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated amylopectin glucan chains, especially medium/long chains
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies/directly forms amylopectin chain structure contributing to target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnSS2 promoter contains an ABRE motif. 2. NnABI5 directly bound the NnSS2 promoter in Y1H and EMSA assays and activated NnSS2pro:LUC in dual-LUC assays. 3. NnSS2 transcript increased in NnABI5-OE lotus roots and decreased in MIR395b-OE lotus roots, linking NnSS2 to enhanced amylopectin biosynthesis and increased medium-/long-chain proportions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes amylopectin biosynthesis and increases medium-/long-chain amylopectin proportion when activated by NnABI5
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter motif analysis; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increased in NnABI5-OE lotus roots and decreased in MIR395b-OE lotus roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient overexpression of NnABI5 or MIR395b in lotus root slices
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as a core pathway gene because it is a direct downstream target experimentally linked to amylopectin traits, although the paper’s main mechanistic focus is the upstream miR395b–NnABI5 module.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Starch branching enzyme; glycoside hydrolase family-associated branching enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces α-1,6 branch points into glucan chains during amylopectin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product structure by creating amylopectin branches
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnSBEII promoter contains an ABRE motif. 2. NnABI5 directly bound the NnSBEII promoter in Y1H and EMSA assays and activated NnSBEIIpro:LUC in dual-LUC assays. 3. NnSBEII transcript increased in NnABI5-OE lotus roots and decreased in MIR395b-OE lotus roots, consistent with higher amylopectin accumulation and altered chain branching.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes amylopectin branching and accumulation when activated by NnABI5
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter motif analysis; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increased in NnABI5-OE lotus roots and decreased in MIR395b-OE lotus roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient overexpression of NnABI5 or MIR395b in lotus root slices
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper identifies NnSBEII as a direct downstream effector of the miR395b–NnABI5 module controlling amylopectin branching.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Amylopectin biosynthesis and branching via repression of transcription factor NnABI5
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional repression and cleavage of target mRNA
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses NnABI5, thereby indirectly reducing expression of NnSS2 and NnSBEII
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator of lotus root amylopectin accumulation and chain-length pattern
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; STTM inhibition; RT-qPCR; iodine staining; starch and amylopectin quantification; ion chromatography of chain length; 5′ RLM-RACE; dual-luciferase assay; cotransformation rescue analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR395b abundance was higher in low-starch cultivar MRH than in high-starch cultivar EL-5. 2. MIR395b overexpression reduced total starch and amylopectin and shifted amylopectin toward more short chains (DP 6–12) and fewer medium/long chains (DP 12–24). 3. STTM inhibition of miR395b had the opposite effect, increasing amylopectin and total starch. 4. miR395b directly cleaves and represses NnABI5 transcripts. 5. In tobacco, MIR395b overexpression also reduced starch and biomass.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates amylopectin biosynthesis, branching pattern, and total starch accumulation in lotus roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in low-starch MRH roots and lower in high-starch EL-5 roots
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Integrated small RNA-seq and transcriptome analysis in lotus roots; tobacco RNA-seq, China National GeneBank DataBase accession CNP0008177
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing lotus roots; transient overexpression or STTM inhibition; transgenic tobacco leaves under controlled growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in tobacco reduced leaf length, width, weight, leaf number, starch content, amylopectin content, and biomass
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppression or editing of miR395b is a potential strategy to increase lotus root starch/amylopectin content and improve rhizome quality and yield; caution that miR395b perturbation may affect hormone signaling and growth traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NnRdRP6 and NnPDR3 were predicted/observed as possible additional responsive genes but not functionally validated as direct nutrient-metabolism core targets in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; ABI5 family; basic leucine zipper domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Amylopectin biosynthesis and chain-length pattern through activation of starch biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding to ABRE motif
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates NnSS2 and NnSBEII transcription by binding ABRE motifs in their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct positive regulator connecting miR395b repression to activation of amylopectin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: miR395b; likely ABA-related context inferred from ABI5 identity but direct upstream ABA regulation not demonstrated here
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Self-activation in yeast was observed, suggesting autonomous transcriptional activation capability; physiological autoregulation not established
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; TRV-mediated silencing; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; iodine staining; starch and amylopectin quantification; chain-length analysis; 5′ RLM-RACE target validation by upstream miRNA; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnABI5 expression is inversely correlated with miR395b and is higher in high-starch EL-5 than in low-starch MRH. 2. NnABI5 is a nuclear bZIP transcription factor and a direct target of miR395b. 3. NnABI5 overexpression increased total starch and amylopectin and raised medium/long amylopectin chain proportions, whereas NnABI5 silencing had the opposite effects. 4. NnABI5 directly binds ABRE motifs in NnSS2 and NnSBEII promoters and activates their expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates amylopectin biosynthesis, branching pattern, and total starch accumulation in lotus roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in high-starch EL-5 than MRH; increased in NnABI5-OE and reduced in NnABI5-TRV or MIR395b-OE lotus roots
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome comparison of EL-5 vs MRH; China National GeneBank DataBase accession CNP0008177 for RNA-seq raw data reported in paper
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing lotus roots; transient overexpression and TRV silencing assays; tobacco transient assays for localization and promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression or activation/editing of NnABI5, potentially combined with downstream pathway genes, could improve lotus root starch quality by increasing amylopectin content and favorable medium/long chain structure; caution that ABI5 is ABA-related and may have pleiotropic developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NnABI5 did not significantly alter NnGBSSIIa or NnSBEI expression in the tested assays; the paper centers on NnSS2 and NnSBEII as direct downstream effectors.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-based transient expression constructs in tobacco/local vectors not fully specified for lotus assays
